item.title: As US Strikes Hit Iran, AI Warfare is Debated At Washington Forum
item.description: Nearly 10,000 targets hit in Iran so far.

item.title: US Recession Risks Begin to Rise as War Dims Economic Outlook
item.description: Wall Street is cutting its forecasts for the US economy this year, boosting its projections for inflation and unemployment and nudging up the odds of a recession as the impact of the Iran war starts to come into view.

item.title: Software Firm Planview Pitches Private Credit to Refinance Debt
item.description: Software firm Planview Inc. has reached out to private credit firms to help it address debt maturing next year, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: Sony Announces New TV and Soundbars as TCL Deal Looms
item.description: Sony Group Corp. introduced a new midrange 4K TV and several home theater audio products, going about business as usual even as it nears a deal to sell a majority stake in its home entertainment business to China’s TCL Electronics Holdings Ltd.

item.title: Mozambique Dollar Bond Selloff Extends as Oil Price Shock Deepens Crisis
item.description: A selloff on Mozambique’s dollar bonds extended into a 10th day, as the oil shock from the Iran war deepens the country’s financial crisis.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Finds Less Treasuries Liquidity on War Volatility
item.description: Interest-rate strategists at Morgan Stanley say this month’s Treasury market slump has hallmarks of forced selling of two-year notes, whose yields soared as traders abandoned wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts and began to price in a hike.

item.title: Musk Deepens Feud With Delaware Judge, Alleging Bias on LinkedIn
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyers are urging the Delaware Chancery Court’s chief judge to step aside from lawsuits involving him and his companies, alleging she has shown bias against the billionaire after ruling against him in high-profile cases.

item.title: ‘Insane’ Volatility Hurts Energy Production Planning, Fed Survey Shows
item.description: Volatility in energy commodity prices driven by the ongoing war in the Middle East is confounding firms trying to make decisions about production in coming months.

item.title: SpaceX Said to Target as Much as $75 Billion in Blockbuster IPO
item.description: SpaceX is considering a fundraising target in its IPO that would dwarf the previous largest ever debut, according to people familiar with the matter, as billionaire Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite maker moves forward with listing plans.

item.title: Air Canada CEO Rebuked by Carney Over English Video on Crash
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney reprimanded Air Canada’s head for issuing a video statement in English only following the tragic runway collision at LaGuardia Airport in New York that killed two pilots.

item.title: UK’s Renewables Output Hits Record, Helping to Blunt Iran Shock
item.description: The UK’s renewables power output hit a record on Wednesday, with a surge in wind and solar generation helping to blunt the impact of the Middle East war on power prices.

item.title: US Demands Iran Dismantle Nuclear Sites as Part of Rejected 15-Point Plan
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s plan to end the war with Iran stipulates that the Islamic Republic dismantle its main nuclear facilities and use a reduced missile arsenal in self-defense only, according to several people familiar with the matter.

item.title: UK Businesses Face Gas Bills Up to 80% Higher on Iran War
item.description: UK businesses face a sharp increase in energy costs as the Iran war drives up wholesale gas and power prices, threatening investments and curbing profits.

item.title: African Nations Assure Residents, Warn Against Hoarding Fuel
item.description: African nations from South Africa to Kenya said they have sufficient fuel and warned against hoarding amid fears of shortages caused by the Iran war, even as counterparts in Asia began rationing and restricting exports.

item.title: Pentagon Wants to Shift Funds to Interceptors as Iran War Drags
item.description: The Pentagon wants to shift roughly $1.5 billion in previously approved funding to buy critical missile interceptors from Lockheed Martin Corp. and RTX Corp., according to the acting comptroller — weapons that are in short supply as the war in Iran consumes vast amounts of the munitions.

item.title: NYC Faces Possible Strike by 34,000 Doormen, Building Workers
item.description: New York City apartment residents are being put on notice of a potential strike by building staff as soon as next month as the union representing the workers and an advisory board to building owners negotiate a new labor contract.

item.title: Franklin Templeton Debuts ETFs That Trade in Crypto Wallets 24/7
item.description: Franklin Templeton is partnering with Ondo Finance to offer tokenized versions of its ETFs that trade around the clock through crypto wallets, bypassing the brokerage accounts and limited trading hours that have defined fund investing for decades.

item.title: Saudi Surges Oil Exports From Yanbu Toward 5 Million Target
item.description: Saudi Arabia has ramped up crude shipments from Yanbu export terminals on the Red Sea coast as it diverts supplies away from the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has kept effectively closed since the end of February.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Seeks to Boost Gas Flows From Algeria
item.description: Giorgia Meloni is seeking to boost gas flows from Algeria, as Italy and Spain vie to secure more North African energy to cushion the loss of Middle Eastern supplies choked off by the Iran war.

item.title: Meta Is Cutting Several Hundred Jobs Amid Record AI Spending
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is cutting several hundred jobs as part of a restructuring effort that’s impacting several teams at the company, including sales, recruiting and the Reality Labs hardware division.

item.title: US Yields Fall With Oil With Focus on Hopes of US-Iran Diplomacy
item.description: Treasuries gained as investors focused on the US’s diplomatic push to end the war with Iran and a related drop in oil prices, despite reports from the country’s semi-official news agency that there wouldn’t yet be a ceasefire.

item.title: Fuel Price Hikes in Chile Test Kast as Supporters Start to Balk
item.description: Fuel pumps are running dry in parts of Chile as drivers rush to tank up before prices jump by as much as 54% Thursday. Angered by the fuel price hikes, some Chileans on Wednesday evening banged pots while student protests briefly shut a key metro line in the capital Santiago.

item.title: BBC Names Ex-Google Executive Brittin to Top Role in Shakeup
item.description: The BBC has named former Google executive Matt Brittin as its new director-general, as the public broadcaster faces challenges to its funding model and a lawsuit from President Donald Trump over a misleading edit.

item.title: SIX Chief Sees Volatility Delaying IPOs, Not Cancelling Them
item.description: The boss of SIX Group AG, the operator of the Swiss and Spanish stock exchanges, is still optimistic that this year could be better for initial public offerings than the last — even as the war in Iran causes companies to rethink plans.

item.title: Apple Rolls Out Age Verification Requirements for UK Users
item.description: Apple Inc. has rolled out age verification requirements for users in the UK that will ask customers to confirm that they’re at least 18 years old to access certain features.

item.title: BOE’s Greene Warns of Lasting Inflation Impact Even If War Ends
item.description: Bank of England rate-setter Megan Greene warned there will be lasting effects for UK inflation even in a “best case” scenario where the conflict in the Middle East swiftly de-escalates.

item.title: Advent Is Said to Earmark Up to $1 Billion for Defense Tech Bets
item.description: Advent is planning to invest as much as $1 billion in the defense sector as global tensions rise, a person familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Energy Fuels Produces ‘Heavy’ Rare Earth Metal at Utah Mill
item.description: Energy Fuels Inc. produced a so-called heavy rare earth element for the first time at its plant in Utah, advancing efforts to build a domestic supply of critical minerals used in electronics and defense technology.

item.title: ‘Lost Cause’ Company Bond Doubles Amid Venezuela All-Out Rally
item.description: A defaulted bond issued by an old Venezuelan power company and long regarded as a lost-cause by investors has almost doubled in value this year as investors look for ways to profit from Venezuela’s new-found rapport with the US.

item.title: UK to Ease Oversight of Big Audit Firms in Starmer’s Growth Push
item.description: The UK’s Financial Reporting Council is set to cut the number of inspections it carries out at major audit firms in what it calls a proportionate approach, as the watchdog pivots toward supporting the government’s economic growth agenda.
